title: Creamy Kenyan Fish Poke
description: A keto-friendly Kenyan dish featuring sashimi-cut fish, citrusy flavors, and a creamy butter sauce, perfect for meal prep. This dish is aerated with a light and refreshing texture.
instructions: Cut the fish into small sashimi-cut pieces and place them in a large bowl.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the softened butter, lime juice, lemon juice, cilantro, ginger, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper until well combined.
Pour the butter sauce over the fish and gently toss to coat.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Just before serving, give the fish a gentle stir to redistribute the sauce.
Serve the fish poke chilled, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
Before serving, make sure the fish is chilled to a safe internal temperature of 40 degrees Fahrenheit or below.
